prompt: I earn £105,000 and my wife doesn't work — she stays home with our two kids and we receive child benefit. I know I'm losing my personal allowance and probably need to pay back child benefit. What's the best way to reduce my tax bill? I have a SIPP and my employer offers salary sacrifice.

assertions.text: Explains personal allowance tapering: £1 lost for every £2 over £100,000 creating 60% effective rate
Calculates or explains the HICBC charge (income between £60,000-£80,000 range for 2024/25)
Recommends pension contributions to bring adjusted net income below £100,000
Explains salary sacrifice saves NI contributions compared to personal pension contributions
Quantifies the amount of pension contribution needed (at least £5,000 to get below £100k)
Mentions marriage allowance eligibility since wife has no income
Provides a quantified estimate of total tax savings from recommended strategies
Includes a disclaimer that this is not professional tax advice
